question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/token/Token.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: setTotalSupply()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly submit token mint/burn transactions that trigger the negative-value aggregation branch (`totalSupply = totalSupply + newTotalSupply`) across thousands of tokens simultaneously, causing the importer's record-item processing loop to stall long enough to delay a block by 500% or more of the 24-hour average block time, effectively freezing network transaction ingestion?
